greeting.sprayFields: {count, plural, one {# field ready to spray} other {# fields ready to spray}}
greeting.tasksToday: {count, plural, one {# task due today} other {# tasks due today}}
greeting.welcome: Welcome back — here's your farm today.

clauses.title: ISO 27001:2022 Clause Tracker
clauses.subtitle: Track your progress through clauses 4–10. All descriptions below are original paraphrases, not ISO text.
clauses.selectClause: Select a clause to view details

assets.title: Farm Assets
assets.listDescription: Every machine, vehicle, building, and piece of equipment on the farm — tracked, owned, and linked to its risks.
assets.name: Name
assets.controlsNote: Tag controls related to this asset for traceability.

risks.title: Risk Register
risks.listDescription: The single register of business risk — track, treat, and evidence.

controls.title: Practices & Controls
controls.listDescription: Every control mapped to its requirements and evidence.
controls.name: Control Name
controls.assetsNote: Tag assets protected by this control for traceability.
controls.justificationPlaceholder: Explain why this control does not apply to your organization...
controls.justificationRequired: Justification is required when marking a control as Not Applicable

evidence.title: Records
evidence.listDescription: Proof for every control — collected, reviewed, and audit-ready.

policies.title: Policy Management
policies.listDescription: Authored, approved, and acknowledged — your live policy library.
policies.selectPolicy: Select a policy to view details

audits.title: Inspections
audits.listDescription: Cycles, packs, and findings — the audit trail your assessors will see.
audits.selectAudit: Select an audit to view details

findings.title: Nonconformity Register
findings.listDescription: Issues raised in audit and assessment — owned, dated, closed.
findings.selectFinding: Select a finding to view details

mapping.title: Scheme Mapping
mapping.subtitle: Cross-framework readiness view. All descriptions are original paraphrases.

admin.title: Administration
admin.templateDescription: Available policy templates to quick-start your compliance documentation.

apiKeys.fullAccessTooltip: Gives this key read + write on every resource — evidence, controls, admin settings. Prefer narrow scopes for automation.
apiKeys.keyCopied: API key copied — paste it into your tool now.
apiKeys.keyCopyFailed: Copy failed — select the key and copy manually.
apiKeys.copyKeyNow: Copy this key now — it will never be shown again!
apiKeys.createdSuccess: API key "{name}" created. Copy the key below.
apiKeys.revokedCount: Revoked {count, plural, one {# API key} other {# API keys}}.
apiKeys.heading: API Keys
apiKeys.description: Manage machine-to-machine API keys for programmatic access. Keys are scoped to specific resources and actions.
apiKeys.expiryTooltip: Keys with no expiry stay valid until someone manually revokes them. Set a deadline for any automation or third-party integration.
apiKeys.confirmRevokeDesc: Key prefix {prefix}… will stop working immediately. Any integration using it will lose access. This cannot be undone.

billing.heading: Billing
billing.description: Manage your workspace plan and billing for {name}.
billing.trialDaysLeft: {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} left in your trial
billing.trialEndsOn: Trial ends on {date}. Upgrade to keep access to premium features.
billing.paymentIssueBody: Please update your payment method to avoid service interruption.
billing.selfHostedBody: This instance runs without a Stripe integration — plan limits resolve to ENTERPRISE for every tenant and the in-app upgrade / portal buttons are disabled. Subscription changes are managed by your operator.
billing.manageSubscriptionBody: Update payment method, view invoices, or change your plan via the Stripe Customer Portal.

entra.heading: Microsoft Entra ID
entra.step1Item1: Create an App Registration in the Entra portal; set the redirect URI to {icUrl}/api/auth/callback/microsoft-entra-id.
entra.step1Item2: In Token configuration, add a groups claim set to Security groups (manifest "groupMembershipClaims": "SecurityGroup"). IC cannot set this for you — without it, no group claims reach IC.
entra.enforceGateHint: When on, a user who matches none of your group → role mappings is denied access. Only takes effect once you've added at least one mapping below.
entra.saveFailedGuid: Save failed — check the directory + client IDs are valid GUIDs.

integrations.consentFlowError: Could not start the SharePoint consent flow.
integrations.spDescription: Source compliance evidence from SharePoint document libraries and keep policies in sync. Connecting grants IC delegated read access to the sites you choose.
integrations.chooseSites: Choose which sites IC may access:
integrations.heading: Integrations
integrations.noIntegrations: No integrations configured. Click "Add Integration" to get started.
integrations.secretsEncrypted: Secrets are encrypted at rest. They cannot be viewed after saving.

ledgerIntegrity.foundBreak: Reconciliation found a hash-chain break — review the report below.
ledgerIntegrity.noRuns: No reconciliation runs yet. Run one to verify the stock ledger.
ledgerIntegrity.neverRunSubtitle: Run a reconciliation to verify the append-only stock ledger's hash chain.
ledgerIntegrity.driftSubtitle: Hash-chain break at entry #{num}. Do not mutate the ledger — see the runbook. Last run {when}.
ledgerIntegrity.unknownSubtitle: Verdict unavailable for this run. Last run {when}.

modules.heading: Modules
modules.description: Turn modules off to hide the parts of the product your team doesn't use. A disabled module's pages and APIs return a permission error until you turn it back on. Leaving everything on is the default.
modules.everyModuleOff: Every module is off — only core admin and settings remain available.

notificationSettings.confirmRunJob: Are you sure you want to run the {job} job now?
notificationSettings.heading: Email Notifications
notificationSettings.bccNote: All outbound emails will be BCC'd to this address.

riskAppetite.heading: Risk Appetite
riskAppetite.simulatedNote: simulated portfolio percentile (falls back to Σ of means until a simulation runs)

roles.baseRoleHint: Used for coarse authorization when custom permissions don't apply.
roles.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional — describe what this role is for
roles.memberWarning: {count} member(s) will lose this custom role and fall back to their base role.
roles.heading: Custom Roles
roles.rolesDefined: {count, plural, one {# custom role} other {# custom roles}} defined. Members assigned a custom role use its permissions instead of the built-in role defaults.
roles.noRoles: No custom roles defined yet. Click "Create Custom Role" to get started.

scim.noPermission: You do not have permission to view SCIM tokens.
scim.tokenCopied: SCIM token copied — paste it into your IdP now.
scim.copyFailed: Copy failed — select the token and copy manually.
scim.heading: SCIM Provisioning
scim.description: Automate user provisioning from your identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin).
scim.endpointHelp: Use this base URL when configuring SCIM in your identity provider.
scim.tokenNotShownAgain: This token will not be shown again. Store it securely in your identity provider.
scim.noActiveTokensHelp: Generate a token to enable automated provisioning from your identity provider.
scim.revokedTokenCount: {count, plural, one {# revoked token} other {# revoked tokens}}
scim.guide1: Generate a SCIM token above and copy it securely.
scim.guide2: In your IdP (Okta, Azure AD, etc.), configure a SCIM 2.0 provisioning connector.
scim.guide3: Set the SCIM connector base URL to the endpoint shown above.
scim.guide4: Set Authentication to "HTTP Header" with the bearer token.
scim.guide5: Enable provisioning actions: Create Users, Update User Attributes, Deactivate Users.
scim.guide6: Test the connection from your IdP's SCIM provisioning settings.
scim.roleMapping: Role mapping: SCIM-provisioned users are assigned the Reader role by default. Editors and Auditors can be mapped via your IdP's group/role assignment. Admin role cannot be assigned via SCIM — it must be set manually.
scim.confirmRevokeDesc: Any IdP using this token will lose access immediately. This cannot be undone.

security.confirmRevokeMine: This will sign you out of all devices. Continue?
security.mySessionsRevoked: Your sessions have been revoked. You will be signed out shortly.
security.confirmRevokeAll: WARNING: This will sign out ALL users in your organization. Are you sure?
security.allSessionsRevoked: Sessions revoked for {count} users. Everyone will need to sign in again.

DISABLED.label: Disabled
DISABLED.description: MFA is not available. Users cannot enroll in multi-factor authentication.

OPTIONAL.label: Optional
OPTIONAL.description: Users can choose to enable MFA. Enrolled users will be challenged at login.

REQUIRED.label: Required
REQUIRED.description: All users must enroll in MFA. Users without MFA will be redirected to enrollment on login.

security.heading: Security & MFA
security.mfaPolicyTooltip: Applies tenant-wide on the next login. Switching to Required forces everyone who isn't enrolled into the enrolment flow before they can access any page.
security.mfaPolicyDesc: Configure whether MFA is required, optional, or disabled for your organization.
security.beforeRequired1: Ensure you (the admin) have enrolled in MFA first
security.beforeRequired2: Users without MFA will be redirected to enrollment on their next login
security.beforeRequired3: Break-glass admin access is preserved via SSO if configured
security.sessionSettingsDesc: Configure session timeout for your organization. Leave blank for the default.
security.maxSessionAgeTooltip: Absolute lifetime of a login session — users must re-authenticate after this many minutes regardless of activity. Leave blank to inherit the platform default.
security.sessionManagementDesc: Revoke active sessions. Revoked users must sign in again.
security.signOutOthersDesc: Invalidate all your active sessions across devices.
security.revokeAllUsersDesc: Force all organization members to sign in again. Use for incidents.
security.revokeSpecificUser: Revoke sessions for a specific user

sso.heading: SSO & Identity
sso.description: Configure enterprise single sign-on for your workspace.
sso.nameIdFormatTooltip: Leave empty if your IdP issues email-format NameIDs (the common case). Override only if your IdP uses a non-standard URN — check the IdP's SAML attribute mapping.
sso.enforceTooltip: Everyone must sign in via your IdP once this saves. Local-password admins keep break-glass access — confirm at least one admin has a working password before enabling.
sso.enforcedNotice: When enforced, users must authenticate via SSO. Only admins with a local password can bypass (break-glass access).
sso.howItWorks1: • Users must already have an account and tenant membership to login via SSO
sso.howItWorks2: • SSO links are created automatically on first successful login
sso.howItWorks3: • When enforced, only admins with a local password can bypass SSO (break-glass)
sso.howItWorks4: • Email domains help auto-discover SSO on the login page
sso.confirmDeleteDesc: Users will need to use local login until SSO is reconfigured. This cannot be undone.

vendorTemplates.heading: Vendor questionnaire templates
vendorTemplates.description: Author and publish reusable assessment templates. Edits on a published template require cloning to a new draft revision.
vendorTemplates.noTemplatesDesc: Use the form above to create your first vendor questionnaire template.

vendorTemplateBuilder.publishedBannerBody: Edits to a published template would invalidate any live assessments pinned to its version. Clone it to a new draft revision to continue editing.
vendorTemplateBuilder.noQuestions: No questions yet — drag here or add one below.
vendorTemplateBuilder.promptPlaceholder: Do you encrypt data at rest?

vendorReview.awaitingRespondentBody: The vendor hasn't submitted yet. Review controls unlock once the assessment moves to SUBMITTED.

members.inviteCreatedNoEmail: Invite created for {email}, but the email could not be sent (check SMTP config). Share this link: {link}
members.confirmDeactivate: Deactivate {email}? They will lose access to this tenant.
members.confirmRevokeSession: Revoke this session? The user will be signed out from this device on their next request.
members.heading: Members & Roles
members.sessionsModalDesc: Live sessions for this member. Revoke any device to sign it out on its next request.
members.activeSessionsCount: {count, plural, one {# active session} other {# active sessions}}.
members.noActiveSessionsDesc: This user is not currently signed in on any device.
members.certificatesDesc: Plant-protection certificates carried by this member — printed on the БАБХ ДНЕВНИК.
members.certificatesEditableNote: Editable per member. Blank clears a field.

riskManager.title: Risks
riskManager.startScratchDesc: Create a custom risk with your own title, description, and scoring.
riskManager.useTemplateDesc: Start from a pre-built risk template with recommended scoring.
riskManager.noTemplates: No templates available. Try creating from scratch.
riskManager.noPermission: You don't have permission to create risks.
riskManager.csvDesc: Upload a CSV file with at minimum a title column. Optional columns: description, category, likelihood (1-5), impact (1-5), owner.
riskManager.noValidRows: No valid rows found. Ensure CSV has a "title" column header.
riskManager.noImportPermission: You don't have permission to import risks.
riskManager.noControlsAvailable: No controls available. Add controls first from the Controls page.

fileUpload.errorDocuments: File type not supported (document files only)
fileUpload.errorProgramResourceImages: File type not supported (.svg, .png, .jpg, or .webp only)

ganttTimeline.emptyMessage: No timeline events in this range.

frameworkBuilder.title: Builder — reorder mode
frameworkBuilder.description: Drag the handle on any row to reorder. Changes are saved per-tenant — the framework stays unchanged for everyone else.

versionDiff.emptyState: Add a second version to enable comparison.
versionDiff.pickTwoDifferent: Pick two different versions to see a diff.
versionDiff.emptyDiff: No textual changes between v{from} and v{to}.

approvalBanner.selfApproval: You can’t approve your own request — another reviewer must decide.
approvalBanner.addCommentAria: Add a comment with your decision
approvalBanner.cannotApproveTitle: Reviewers cannot approve their own request
approvalBanner.cannotRejectTitle: Reviewers cannot reject their own request

truncationBanner.refineFilters: Refine your filters to narrow the view and see the rest.

aiAssistRail.intro: Let AI surface candidate risks from your asset inventory — each one scored, with a rationale and suggested controls.

modal.discardDescription: Your unsaved changes will be lost.

ruleBuilder.eventFieldsHint: This event has no filterable fields — the rule fires on every occurrence.
ruleBuilder.setOperatorsHint: Set operators (any of / none of) take a comma-separated value set. Numeric fields support greater/less than.
ruleBuilder.updateStatusHint: Move an entity to a status
ruleBuilder.slaDescription: Optional. If an execution runs past this many minutes it's flagged as breached.
ruleBuilder.chainDescription: Optional. After this rule succeeds, fire another rule.

reject.self: A step can't connect to itself.
reject.annotation: Annotations don't carry the flow — they sit beside it.

persistedCanvas.scTab: Tab — create a connected sibling node
persistedCanvas.cmdDuplicateDesc: Clone the current graph into a new map
persistedCanvas.cmdNewAutomationDesc: Start a visual rule editor (automation) canvas

persistedCanvas.cmdArrangeTbDesc: Auto-layout the canvas with a top-to-bottom flow
persistedCanvas.cmdArrangeSelLrDesc: Auto-layout only the selected nodes — left-to-right
persistedCanvas.cmdArrangeSelTbDesc: Auto-layout only the selected nodes — top-to-bottom
persistedCanvas.cmdForceDesc: Auto-layout the canvas as an organic spring-simulated graph
persistedCanvas.cmdForceSelDesc: Apply force-directed layout to only the selected nodes
persistedCanvas.groupDesc: Wrap the selected nodes in a group container
persistedCanvas.ungroupDesc: Dissolve the selected group + lift children
persistedCanvas.cmdDeleteDesc: Remove every selected node + its edges

persistedCanvas.emptyTitle: Map a business or IT process.
persistedCanvas.emptyBody: Capture the steps, mark the controls between them, and annotate the risks and assets each step touches.

controlExceptions.renewBodyState: state with the same justification + compensating control as this one. The original record is preserved as part of the audit trail. An admin will need to approve the renewal before it takes effect.

testSchedule.cadenceOffDesc: Test runs are created on demand. No auto-schedule.

automationSuggestions.intro: Automations that would close gaps in your current posture — each scored, with a rationale. Create one as a draft, then refine it.
automationSuggestions.noSuggestions: No new suggestions — your enabled rules already cover the obvious gaps.

onboarding.bannerDesc: Finish the onboarding wizard to configure your compliance workspace.
onboarding.accessRestrictedDesc: Only tenant administrators can access the onboarding wizard.
onboarding.workspaceReady: Your workspace is ready. Redirecting to dashboard...
onboarding.welcome: Welcome! Let's set up your workspace.
onboarding.welcomeDesc: This wizard will guide you through configuring your compliance platform in just a few steps.
onboarding.onboardingCompleteDesc: Your workspace has been configured. You can always update settings from the admin panel.
onboarding.setupWizardDesc: Complete these steps to configure your compliance workspace.
onboarding.companyProfileIntro: Tell us about your organization. This information helps tailor your compliance experience.
onboarding.frameworkIntro: Select the compliance frameworks you want to implement. You can add more later.
onboarding.fwIsoDesc: International information security management standard. Installs 93 controls across 4 domains.
onboarding.fwNis2Desc: EU cybersecurity directive for essential and important entities. Installs sector-specific requirements.
onboarding.selectAtLeastOne: Select at least one framework to continue.
onboarding.assetIntro: Add your key information assets. These are the systems, databases, and services you need to protect.
onboarding.assetHint: You can import assets in bulk later from the Assets page.
onboarding.controlIntro: We'll install baseline controls from your selected frameworks. This creates your initial control register.
onboarding.noFrameworksSelected: No frameworks selected. Go back to the Frameworks step to select at least one.
onboarding.riskIntro: Generate a starter risk register based on your assets and selected frameworks.
onboarding.starterRiskDesc: We'll generate common information security risks based on industry best practices. You can customize, add, or remove risks later.
onboarding.teamIntro: Invite your team members. They'll receive an email invitation to join your workspace.
onboarding.teamHint: You can skip this step and invite team members later from the Admin panel.
onboarding.reviewIntro: Review your setup before completing onboarding.
onboarding.reviewNotice: Click "Complete Setup" below to finish onboarding and go to your dashboard.

installPrompt.installDesc: Add it to your home screen — works in the field, online or off.
installPrompt.a2hsAria: Add AgriSaaS to your home screen
installPrompt.a2hsDesc: Tap the Share icon, then “Add to Home Screen” to install AgriSaaS for offline field use.
